required_skills: User Research Methods including Interviews, Surveys, and Contextual Inquiry
Information Architecture and User Flow Design
Interaction Design Principles and Behavioral Psychology
Journey Mapping and Service Blueprint Creation
Design Systems Contribution and Component Library Usage
Cross-functional Collaboration with Product and Engineering
preferred_skills: Quantitative UX research methods including surveys and analytics interpretation
Familiarity with front-end technologies such as HTML and CSS
Participatory design and co-creation workshop facilitation
Eye-tracking or biometric usability research experience
Experience with AI-powered product feature design
responsibilities: Plan and conduct qualitative user research including in-depth interviews, contextual inquiries, and diary studies to uncover deep user needs and mental models
Synthesize research findings into actionable UX artifacts including personas, empathy maps, user journey maps, and service blueprints shared across product teams
Design low-fidelity wireframes and high-fidelity interactive prototypes using Figma to communicate user flows and interaction patterns to stakeholders
Facilitate moderated and unmoderated usability testing sessions using tools such as Maze or UserTesting to validate designs and surface friction points
Define and document information architecture, site maps, and navigation hierarchies that support intuitive and efficient user task completion
Conduct heuristic evaluations and cognitive walkthroughs of existing product experiences to identify usability violations and prioritize UX improvements
Collaborate with UI designers and engineers to ensure design intent is accurately implemented, conducting design QA reviews before and after feature releases
Contribute to the evolution of the product design system by defining UX patterns, interaction guidelines, and component usage documentation
Advocate for accessibility and inclusive design by ensuring all UX deliverables meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards and support diverse user needs
Present research findings, design rationale, and UX recommendations to product managers, engineers, and executive stakeholders with clarity and confidence
